popularStory.headline: William Carpenter of Rehoboth
popularStory.description: William Carpenter married Abigail Briant. William was born ca 1605 in Wiltshire, England and died 7 February 1658/9 in Rehoboth, Massachusetts. His estate inventory was 21 February 1658/9? and the wil …

zh.lifeSummary: William Carpenter是William Carpenter的兒子，於大約1605年出生在英國維爾爾沙爾布恩。他於1625年4月28日與Abigail Briant在英國維爾爾沙爾布恩結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1644年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼布里斯托雷沃斯。他去世於1659年在美國緬因州安德洛斯可金列維斯頓享年55歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加羅德 易曼德普洛維登斯普洛維登斯紐曼 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Carpenter是William Carpenter的儿子，于大约1605年出生在英国維爾爾沙爾布恩。他于1625年4月28日与Abigail Briant在英国維爾爾沙爾布恩结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1644年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼布里斯托雷沃斯。他去世于1659年在美国缅因州安德洛斯可金列維斯頓享年55岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加羅德 易曼德普洛維登斯普洛維登斯紐曼 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Carpenter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): occupational name for a worker in wood, Norman French carpentier (from Late Latin carpentarius ‘cartwright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Zimmermann  , French   Charpentier  , Italian   Carpentieri  , or cognates and equivalents in various other languages.

variants.name: Zimmermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Hans, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Klaus, Gerd, Manfred, Wolfgang, Bernhard.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a carpenter, Middle High German zimberman (a compound of zimber, zim(m)er ‘timber, wood’ + man ‘man’), German Zimmermann.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: Providence Founded
historicEvents.description: In 1636 Providence is founded as a settlement for Rhode Island by Roger Williams

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: William Carpenter was born about 1605, in Shalbourne, Wiltshire, England as the son of William Carpenter. He married Abigail Briant on 28 April 1625, in Shalbourne, Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He lived in Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America in 1644. He died in 1659, in Lewiston, Androscoggin, Maine, United States, at the age of 55, and was buried in Newman Cemetery, Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: William Carpenter est né environ 1605, à Shalbourne, Wiltshire, Angleterre, fils de William Carpenter. Il a épousé Abigail Briant le 28 avril 1625, à Shalbourne, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il a vécu à Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1644. Il est décédé en 1659, à Lewiston, Androscoggin, Maine, États-Unis, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Newman Cemetery, Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: William Carpenter wurde um 1605 in Shalbourne, Wiltshire, England, als Sohn von William Carpenter geboren. Er hat am 28. April 1625 in Shalbourne, Wiltshire, England Abigail Briant geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1644 hat er in Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America gelebt. Er starb 1659 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Lewiston, Androscoggin, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Newman Cemetery, Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: William Carpenter è nato il circa 1605 nel Shalbourne, Wiltshire, England come figlio di William Carpenter. Lui sposato Abigail Briant il 28 aprile 1625, nel Shalbourne, Wiltshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Vissuto a Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America nel 1644. Morì nel 1659, nel Lewiston, Androscoggin, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Newman Cemetery, Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: William Carpenterは約1605年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアシャルボウルンでWilliam Carpenterの息子として生まれた。1625年4月28日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアシャルボウルンでAbigail Briantと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1644年にはイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーブリストルレhオボスに住んでいた。彼は1659年にアメリカ合衆国メイン州アンドロスコグジンルイストンで55歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカロード イスランドプロヴィデンスプロヴィデンスニューマン セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Carpenter(은)는 대략 1605년 영국 윌츠히르 샤보우른에서 William Carpenter의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1625년 4월 28일 영국 윌츠히르 샤보우른에서 Abigail Briant(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1644년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 브리스톨 레홉스에서 살았다. 그는 1659년 미국 메인 주 앤드로스코긴 루이스턴에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 호드 이슬랜드 프로비데스 프로비데스 뉴먼 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Carpenter nasceu aproximadamente 1605, em Shalbourne, Wiltshire, Inglaterra filho de William Carpenter. Ele casou-se com Abigail Briant em 28 de abril de 1625, em Shalbourne, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele viveu em Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America em 1644. Ele faleceu em 1659, em Lewiston, Androscoggin, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Newman Cemetery, Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: William Carpenter родился около 1605 г. в местности шэлбурн, уилцхир, Англия. Его отца звали William Carpenter. Он женился на женщине по имени Abigail Briant 28 апреля 1625 г. в местности шэлбурн, уилцхир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он жил в местности рэобот, бристол, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1644 году. Он умер в 1659 г. в местности львистон, андроскогин, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности нюман кеметири, провиденс, провиденс, род исланд, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: William Carpenter nació aproximadamente 1605, en Shalbourne, Wiltshire, Inglaterra como hijo de William Carpenter. Se casó con Abigail Briant el 28 de abril de 1625, en Shalbourne, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Vivió en Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America en 1644. Murió en 1659, en Lewiston, Androscoggin, Maine, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Newman Cemetery, Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, British Colonial America.
